content:
# pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Overview

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- New feature development
- Major refactoring with architecture changes
- Web projects with UI and browser testing needs
- Any project where code quality matters more than speed

Inputs required:
- Clear problem statement from Think stage
- Architecture design from Plan stage
- Test strategy defined before Build

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Execution Steps

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Think: Redefine the real problem; answer 6 mandatory questions; get user confirmation
- Plan: Design architecture with data flow, test matrix, tech choices; pass required review gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX as applicable)
- Build: Implement per plan with tests; use WIP checkpoints; boil the lake (do the complete thing)
- Review: Multi-layer code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as risk requires)
- Test: Browser QA for UI projects, regression tests, security scan for high-risk projects
- Ship: Sync main→test→push→PR→merge→deploy→health check→canary→document
- Reflect: Write retro entry; distill learnings into rules/checklists/templates; update database

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Never skip from user request directly to code (Think→Plan first)
- Each stage output feeds the next
- Stop conditions must be met before proceeding
- User confirms direction at Think and Plan before Build begins

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.

content:
# pattern-think-plan-build-review-test-ship-reflect - Failure Modes

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Jumping to Build before Think/Plan complete
- Skipping stages because of time pressure
- Not recording retro (knowledge lost for future agents)

AI summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.
